textPlain: You guys using convoluted git commands when a single line of subversion works:svn checkout https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/trunk/tensorflow/ex...

There's hardly any Rust in there. It's shelling out to the git command. This could have been a couple lines of bash.Actually doing this in Rust with lower-level libraries like gix would have been interesting.

$ git clone --no-checkout $URL/repo.git
   $ cd repo/
   $ git sparse-checkout init
   $ git sparse-checkout set subdirectory_i_want
   $ git checkout main

Same can be done using git and tar    mkdir -p <out_dir> && git archive --remote=<remote> --format=tar.gz <branch> <files...> | tar -xzC <out_dir>

Strangely, github does not support this, so tested with bitbucket.

So,  $ git-down -d bootstrap-dist https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap.git:master dist

is *way* better than  $ git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/twbs/boostrap.git
  $ cd bootstrap
  $ mv ./dist ~/stuff/boostrap-latest 

because: "C'mon, you don't have the time to be doing all that."And then we wonder how the fuck we end with malware in our systems.

I have a similar problem with hugging face. I do git clone and it doesn't download models. I know it's supposed to use LFS, but I don't know how to make it work, I tried everything. I had to install their disgusting Python CLI to download a model.
The rust app just calls a few git commands too[1]Could've been a shell script[2][1] https://github.com/zikani03/git-down/blob/cb2763020edc81e464...[2]  https://textbin.net/ja17q8vga4

Now I'm curious -- is that here a way to do this that avoids downloading any more than strictly necessary?The command above downloads the whole repo history. You could do a depth=1 to skip the history, but it still downloads the he latest version of the entire repo tree.
All that's missing is `curl ... | sudo bash` as install instruction in the README
Don't you just need to install git-lfs https://git-lfs.com/ and then run `git lfs pull` ?
